Title: LAZY3 interacts with LAZY2 to regulate tiller angle by modulating shoot gravity perception in rice

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot gravity-sensing tissues including leaf sheath bases and leaf sheath pulvinus amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides statolith mass for gravity perception, enabling shoot gravitropism and proper tiller angle
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidial starch biosynthesis in gravity-sensing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-6-phosphate to glucose-1-phosphate in plastids, supplying precursor for ADP-glucose production and starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for starch biosynthesis in gravity-sensing tissues and thereby proper shoot gravitropism and tiller angle
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OspPGM was previously shown by the authors to regulate starch biosynthesis in rice gravity-sensing tissues. 2. In this paper OspPGM is positioned in the same functional module with LA2 and LA3, likely within a LA3-LA2-OspPGM complex. 3. OspPGM is treated here as a general starch-biosynthetic enzyme rather than the novel core subject experimentally dissected in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast/plastid (as discussed for plastidial starch biosynthesis)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LA2 (previous study); no interaction with LA3 detected in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior mutant/interaction evidence; pathway inference in current paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as part of the proposed LA3-LA2-OspPGM complex, but not experimentally established as a new core gene in the Results of this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with gravity-sensing starch regulators for architecture engineering, but evidence in this paper is indirect.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As a general starch biosynthesis enzyme, perturbation may affect broader starch metabolism across tissues, though not tested here.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot gravity-sensing tissues, especially leaf sheath bases at seedling stage and leaf sheath pulvinus at adult stage
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides statolith-based gravity sensing needed for architecture control
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: starch biosynthesis regulator / starch-granule-associated regulatory protein
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch biosynthesis and starch granule formation in gravity-sensing tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated positive regulation of starch granule formation; acts upstream of LA1-mediated auxin asymmetry
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LA2-associated starch biosynthesis module; indirectly LA1-mediated asymmetric auxin distribution
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes starch granule formation with LA2; positively enables downstream LA1-dependent auxin asymmetry upon gravistimulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of starch granule biogenesis in rice shoot gravity-sensing tissues
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gravistimulation context; intrinsic gravity-sensing tissue program
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with LA2; proposed component of LA3-LA2-OspPGM complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of LA3 abolishes starch granules in shoot gravity-sensing tissues, reduces shoot gravitropism, impairs asymmetric auxin distribution, and increases tiller angle
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LA3 encodes a chloroplast-localized tryptophan-rich protein associated with starch granules. 2. LA3 is indispensable for starch biosynthesis in rice shoot gravity-sensing tissues, with la3 mutants lacking detectable starch granules in leaf sheath bases and pulvinus. 3. LA3 interacts with LA2, acts in the same pathway upstream of LA1-mediated auxin asymmetry, and thereby negatively regulates tiller angle by promoting gravitropism.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tryptophan-rich protein; ESV1 homolog; contains chloroplast transit peptide, TRR domain, and PRR domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; subcellular localization; truncation localization analysis; starch staining; qRT-PCR of auxin markers; LCI; BiFC; genetic interaction analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter for complementation; 35S constitutive promoter for transient localization, LCI and BiFC assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Gravistimulation; seedling and adult shoot gravity-sensing tissues
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LA3 association with starch granules depends on two TRR subdomains (G211-G244 and S278-W313); likely binds starch via WWE(K/R)W motifs. Grain filling and yield-related traits were not significantly affected in la3.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to optimize rice tiller angle and plant architecture without major penalties on yield traits; useful for breeding ideal architecture via gravity-sensing pathway modulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalties in tiller number, plant height, panicle traits, grain size, 1000-grain weight, or grain yield per plant were detected in la3, but loose architecture occurs in loss-of-function plants.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot gravity-sensing tissues including leaf sheath bases/pulvinus amyloplasts
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports gravity perception for architecture control
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated regulation via OspPGM and LA3
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OspPGM; LA3-associated starch granule formation module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes starch biosynthesis in gravity-sensing tissues
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential regulator of starch granule formation in gravity-sensing tissues, acting in the same pathway as LA3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with LA3 in this paper; previously shown to interact with OspPGM; proposed LA3-LA2-OspPGM complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of LA2 abolishes starch granules in gravity-sensing tissues, reduces gravitropism, and increases tiller angle
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LA2 physically interacts with LA3 on starch granules in chloroplasts. 2. LA2 and LA3 act in the same pathway for starch granule formation, shoot gravitropism and tiller angle regulation. 3. LA2 is positioned upstream or in concert with LA3 and together with OspPGM forms the proposed starch-biosynthetic regulatory complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LCI; BiFC; double-mutant analysis with la3; referenced previous functional genetics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the mechanism, most direct functional characterization of LA2 was reported previously; this paper mainly extends its interaction and pathway relationship with LA3.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential architecture-engineering target within gravity-sensing starch pathway, especially in combination with LA3.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Asymmetrically distributed in shoot bases upon gravistimulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Drives differential growth for gravitropic curvature
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lateral auxin transport / asymmetric auxin distribution during gravity response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts downstream of starch-statolith gravity perception to mediate auxin asymmetry
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Auxin redistribution outputs reflected by OsIAA20, WOX6, WOX11 asymmetry
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream mediator linking gravity perception to auxin asymmetry and tiller angle
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Starch-statolith-dependent gravity sensing via LA3-LA2-OspPGM pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: la1 is epistatic to la3 for shoot gravitropism and tiller angle, indicating LA1 acts downstream of LA3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Genetic analysis showed CR-la3-6 la1 resembles la1, demonstrating that LA3 acts upstream of LA1. 2. LA1 functions downstream of starch-statolith-dependent gravity perception. 3. LA1 mediates the auxin asymmetry that controls shoot gravitropism and tiller angle.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LA1 is not newly cloned here but is a core pathway gene in the Results through genetic epistasis establishing pathway order.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential major architecture regulator, but stronger pleiotropic effects than LA3 may need caution.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely strong architecture effects; specific tradeoffs not reported here.
